description: Web platform hardening — scoped to stories that are build/typecheck/test-verifiable in a Windows/no-Xcode loop. iOS-native and prod-deploy stories intentionally excluded.

userStories.title: Make the domain-context split deliver real re-render isolation instead of routing everything through useApp()
userStories.description: As a performance engineer, I want components to subscribe only to the data they use so that a single grocery toggle or food edit does not re-render all 43 useApp() consumers, completing the value the AppContext split was supposed to provide.
acceptanceCriteria: Audit the ~43 components/pages using useApp(); migrate each to the narrowest domain hook(s) it needs (useFoods/useKids/useRecipes/usePlan/useGrocery) where practical
Split the merged context value so unrelated domains don't share one ~40-key memoized object that rebuilds on every change (AppContext.tsx ~:348-372 / AppContextComposer ~:82-88)
Memoize per-domain context values and stabilize callback identities (e.g. exportData at ~:320 should not get a new reference on every data change)
Add a lightweight render-count test or React Profiler note demonstrating that toggling a grocery item no longer re-renders foods-only components
No behavior regressions in Pantry/Grocery/Planner/Recipes/Dashboard
Typecheck passes; tests pass
userStories.notes: Audit: 43 components consume the merged useApp(); domain hooks are only used inside the contexts, so the US-012 split currently yields zero isolation. Follows up US-012.

userStories.title: Define and document localStorage vs Supabase precedence and conflict handling on load
userStories.description: As an engineer, I want a clear, tested rule for which source wins when the localStorage backup diverges from Supabase so that cross-device edits don't silently resurrect stale data.
acceptanceCriteria: Document the intended precedence (server-authoritative on load, localStorage only as offline fallback) in CLAUDE.md / code comments
Audit AppContext load logic: ensure a successful Supabase fetch overwrites (not merges-stale) the local cache, using updated_at where available for conflict resolution
Ensure offline edits queued locally are reconciled against server state on reconnect without clobbering newer server rows (coordinate with syncQueue)
Add tests for: server-newer wins on load; local offline edit survives reconnect; stale local does not overwrite newer server data
Typecheck passes; tests pass
userStories.notes: Audit: localStorage vs Supabase precedence on load is unclear; divergence/conflict handling not explicitly defined.

userStories.title: Eliminate `any` usage and ban it via lint to restore type safety
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the ~307 `any` usages removed and the rule enforced so that the 'no any' rule in CLAUDE.md is actually upheld and type errors surface at build time.
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory all `: any`, `<any>`, and `as any` occurrences in src (~307) and replace with precise types (use Database['public']['Tables'][...] Row/Insert types where applicable)
Reduce remaining @ts-ignore/@ts-expect-error (~30) by fixing the underlying types; keep only justified, commented suppressions
Enable @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any (error) and no-non-null-assertion guidance in eslint.config.js
Land incrementally if needed (per-directory) so each PR stays reviewable; CI typecheck (US-329) must be real first
npm run lint passes with the new rule; typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Verified counts: 307 any-style usages, 30 ts-ignore/expect-error in src.

userStories.title: Replace remaining select('*') over-fetches on hot paths with column-specific selects
userStories.description: As a performance engineer, I want hot-path Supabase reads to select only needed columns so that we cut payload size and avoid pulling unused/large fields, finishing the work started in US-014 (~182 select('*') remain).
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory the ~182 remaining .select('*') calls; prioritize high-frequency/large-table reads (foods, recipes, grocery_items, plan_entries, kids, admin dashboards)
Replace with explicit column lists for the prioritized reads; leave low-traffic/admin one-offs documented if intentionally left
Verify no code depends on a column that was dropped from the select (typecheck + runtime spot check)
Confirm RLS-relevant columns (user_id/household_id) remain selected where filtering/normalization needs them
Typecheck passes; tests pass
userStories.notes: Verified: 182 select('*') occurrences remain after US-014.

userStories.title: Dark-mode regression sweep: replace remaining hardcoded colors with theme tokens
userStories.description: As a designer/engineer, I want the ~112 remaining hardcoded color utilities (bg-gray-*, text-gray-*, bg-white, text-black, hex) outside src/components/ui replaced with semantic tokens so that dark mode stays consistent in components added since US-022/US-098..108.
acceptanceCriteria: Inventory hardcoded colors in src/pages and src/components (excluding src/components/ui shadcn) — ~112 occurrences
Replace with semantic Tailwind tokens (bg-background, text-foreground, bg-muted, border-border, etc.) per the existing convention
Manually verify the touched screens in both light and dark mode (note any intentionally-fixed colors, e.g. brand logos, and exclude them)
No use of raw hex where a token exists
Typecheck passes; npm run build succeeds
userStories.notes: Verified: ~112 hardcoded-color utilities remain outside ui/ despite prior dark-mode fixes.
